characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The protagonist ofSilverwingandSunwing. A small yet fierce Silverwing whose thirst for adventure and willingness to question tradition propel him to glory. Fantastic Racism: His disgust for Vampyrum Spectrum goes beyond that warranted by self-preservation. When he meets a Vampyrum who doesn't attack him and in fact goes to great lengths to help him, he still doesn't trust him. In Harm's Way: After defeating Goth, he finds himself bored by the peaceful repetition of ordinary life (and separation from his loved ones). When an opportunity to travel again presents itself, he eagerly takes it.

characters.name: Shade
characters.description: The protagonist ofSilverwingandSunwing. A small yet fierce Silverwing whose thirst for adventure and willingness to question tradition propel him to glory. Fantastic Racism: His disgust for Vampyrum Spectrum goes beyond that warranted by self-preservation. When he meets a Vampyrum who doesn't attack him and in fact goes to great lengths to help him, he still doesn't trust him. In Harm's Way: After defeating Goth, he finds himself bored by the peaceful repetition of ordinary life (and separation from his loved ones). When an opportunity to travel again presents itself, he eagerly takes it.

characters.name: Marina
characters.description: ThedeuteragonistofSilverwingandSunwing. An independent, emotionally expressive Brightwing who was exiled unfairly from her colony. Deadpan Snarker: Often replies to Shade'sCaptain Obviouscomments with a sarcastic remark. Operation: Jealousy: She flirts with Chinook to get a reaction out of Shade. Women Are Wiser: Not that Shade is dumb, but Marina immediately figures out that Goth and Throbb are bad news.

characters.name: Griffin
characters.description: The protagonist ofFirewing. A timid newborn whose hybrid Silverwing/Brightwing ancestry makes him look odd. He's the son of Marina and the great hero Shade, a legacy he knows he can't live up to. This insecurity, among other things, inadvertently causes the death of his best friend. The Atoner: Being cast into the underworld is almost agood thingfor Griffin, because it gives him a way to help the victim of his negligence- and, in some inadequate way, make up for what he took from her. He lies to her about how she died because he wants to alleviate his feelings of guilt, and his final trial is to admit to her that it was his fault. Mr. Imagination: Downplayed. He is fully aware of reality, but fear sends him on unrealistic tangents about everything thatcouldgo wrong in a given situation. When he tries to direct his creativity away from pessimism, he's actually a very good storyteller. Undying Loyalty: He flies through hell, quite literally, for Luna.

characters.name: Luna
characters.description: One ofFirewing's deuteragonists. A courageous Silverwing newborn whose tragic death was a great loss to her whole colony. Her journey through the underworld- and growing bond with Griffin, her childhood friend- forms the main plot of the book. Death Is Dramatic: The subversion of this trope in regards to Lunainfuriatesher: she died torturously and pointlessly because of a stupid mistake made by someone else. Her final trial is to cope with the injustice of it withoutabandoning her moralityin the process. Moment of Weakness: After learning about Griffin's role in her death, Luna goes briefly insane and tries to kill him, screaming insults completely contrary to her true personality. She snaps out of it just moments later, as horrified by her victim by what she had done. Nice Girl: She hung out with Griffin even though he was much less cool than her, and never made him feel pitied or insecure. Her compassion runs so deep that even in the underworld, as anamnesiac ghost, she leaves a colony she wanted to stay with because she can't stand how they're treating Griffin.

characters.name: Zephyr
characters.description: Keeper of the Spire - a blind, albino bat living in a city cathedral. He helps Shade and Marina on their journey. Blind Seer: He can see the past, present, and future in faint echoes. Disability Superpower: Despite being a blind albino, he can hear everything, even stars.

characters.name: Goth
characters.description: The trilogy's villain, a hulking cannibal bat who drives his kingdom-slash-cult to resurrect the dark god Cama Zotz. Aristocrats Are Evil: He's prince of the Vampyrum Spectrumand later king when his father dies. Evil Is Bigger: Being a spectral bat, the largest bat in the Americas, he towers over the protagonists. Genius Bruiser: Goth's a big bat. But, he's a skilled manipulator who's virtually unkillable. Hoist by His Own Petard: Wearing all the metal bands he took off the banded bats as trophies causes him to be struck by lightning.He survives, albeit severely weakened.

characters.name: Throbb
characters.description: Goth's acquaintance, and also a Vampyrum Spectrum. Evil Is Bigger: He's not as large or strong as Goth, but he's still a spectral bat, and thus larger than the protagonists. Hoist by His Own Petard: Wearing all the metal bands he took off the banded bats as trophies causes him to be struck by lightning.This results inNo Body Left Behindas he's disintegrated. Kill It with Ice: Not being used to the cold of the north, he gets frostbite on one of his wings.He doesn't live long enough for it to kill him.

characters.name: Romulus
characters.description: The prince, later king,of the rats and older brother of Remus. Freakiness Shame: His brother calls him a freak because he possesses patagium - flaps of skin on his limbs, like a flying squirrel. The overly paranoid prince of the rats and younger brother of Romulus. Adipose Rex: He's very big, very fat, and rather unhygienic. Body Horror: In the graphic novel, he's affected with mange.

characters.name: Java
characters.description: A friendly Foxwing who died of old age. Gentle Giant: Being a flying fox, she towers over the other characters. She's also the nicest of the pilgrims.

characters.name: Murk
characters.description: A helpful Vampyrum Spectrum who wants to escape the Underworld. I Am What I Am: Makes no excuses for his species' diet or other habits. White Sheep: The only spectral bat that acts truly friendly.

characters.name: Phoenix
characters.description: A large Vampyrum Spectrum who does the bidding of Cama Zotz. Large and in Charge: She's the Chief Builder directly under Cama Zotz. It's noted that she's even larger than Goth. More Deadly Than the Male: Cama Zotz himself says that Phoenix is Goth's equal in every way, but more relentless.
